claim: No Stable, release, conformance, adapter-safety, package-parity, or recorded manual assistive-technology claim is made by this source contract.

semantics.nativeBasis: native input type=text and hidden raw or formatted form input
semantics.naming: A persistent visible Field label, native label, aria-label, or aria-labelledby names the visible editable input; localized description and error relationships remain attached.
semantics.roleOverride: Consumer role overrides are outside the supported contract.

interaction.keyboard: The browser owns editing, selection, undo, clipboard, composition, and validation. The component intercepts no browser, screen-reader, editor, or platform key.
interaction.pointer: Native input selection and pointer cancellation remain authoritative; the trusted adapter maps caret offsets after formatting.
interaction.touch: The native input preserves a 44 CSS pixel target and consumer-selected mobile inputMode appropriate to the masked data.
interaction.speech: The persistent visible label remains available for voice targeting and mask instructions belong in localized visible description text.
interaction.switchAccess: The real input is the only interactive stop produced by the component.

formBehavior: The visible formatted input owns native required and custom adapter validity but intentionally has no form name. An optional hidden same-form control serializes either raw or formatted value according to the explicit serialization prop. Disabled fields are omitted. Uncontrolled reset restores the default; controlled values remain consumer-owned.

states.synchronization: Visible formatted text, raw value, chosen serialized value, adapter status, custom validity, Field relationships, and controlled or uncontrolled ownership update together; composition is not transformed mid-sequence.

preferences.enhancedContrast: Validation includes programmatic state and text rather than color alone.
preferences.forcedColors: Uses system colors, a visible double invalid border, and an explicit focus indicator.
preferences.reducedMotion: No value, validation, caret, or layout behavior depends on animation.

responsive.overflowPolicy: The input and localized validation text use intrinsic width, wrapping, logical spacing, and no fixed text height.

internationalization.imePolicy: Composition text remains exact and unformatted until compositionend; no key, beforeinput, or paste event is canceled.
internationalization.serialization: The public value reports both raw and formatted text. Form serialization is an explicit raw or formatted choice and never silently changes by locale.

consumerResponsibilities: Import a deterministic mask adapter from trusted reviewed application code and keep its work bounded by maxInputLength.
Do not deserialize, compile, eval, dynamically import, or execute mask expressions, regular-expression strings, registry metadata, URL state, or user input.
Choose and document whether the server expects raw or formatted serialization; validate it again on the server.
Provide a persistent visible label and localized format instructions that do not rely on placeholder text alone.
Select inputMode, autocomplete, and adapter character policy appropriate to the actual field purpose and locale.
limitations: Mergora deliberately supplies an adapter boundary rather than a universal declarative mask language; consumers must review and test the trusted adapter for their data and locale.
A synchronous JavaScript adapter can still be defective; bounded input and output prevent unbounded data size but cannot preempt blocking code inside trusted application modules.

Source-present-unreleased records do not include immutable generated artifacts, production adapter evidence, packed-consumer evidence, package/source parity, or current manual assistive-technology sessions.
